about.title: 1. About UrbanDrop
content: UrbanDrop is an online marketplace that connects customers with independent merchants offering groceries, meals, and ethnic products for delivery or collection. We provide the digital infrastructure, payment processing, and customer interface, but we do not produce, own, or sell the items listed on the Platform.
When you place an order through UrbanDrop, you are purchasing directly from the merchant, not from UrbanDrop.

hero.desc: UrbanDrop is dedicated to bringing the richness of ethnic cuisines to your doorstep while supporting local merchants and celebrating cultural diversity.

chapter1.label: The Gap
chapter1.title: Something was quietly <1>missing</1>.
chapter1.p1: When I moved from Ghana to the UK in 2022, I arrived with hope and a deep connection to the culture I was leaving behind. It didn't take long to feel what so many immigrants silently endure.
chapter1.p2: Shopping for Ghanaian groceries—anything African or Caribbean—meant <1>long trips to scattered shops</1>, limited selections, and almost no convenience. Mainstream delivery apps served UK and European brands with ease. But nothing truly served ethnic communities—the very people enriching this country every single day.

chapter2.label: The Moment
chapter2.title: A bus ride that <1>changed everything</1>.
chapter2.p1: One evening, my wife's mother made the journey to shop at an African grocery store after a long day. Exhausted on the bus ride home, she stepped off and left every bag behind without realising it.
chapter2.quote: It wasn't just about missing food from home. It was about how hard people work, how far they travel simply to stay connected to who they are.

chapter3.label: The Mission
chapter3.title: That's where <1>Urbandrop</1> was born.
chapter3.p1: We are building a digital marketplace so Afro-Caribbean, Asian, and other ethnic communities can shop for the food they love right from their phones. <1>No long trips. No heavy bags.</1> Fresh, familiar products delivered with care and dignity.

closing.quote: Food is more than ingredients; it's memory, comfort, identity. In a country that has welcomed so many, <1>they deserve a better way to feel at home</1>. This is our mission. And we are just getting started.

items.question: How do I create an account?
items.answer: To create an account, click on the 'Sign Up' button at the top right corner of the home page. Fill in your details including your name, email address, and a secure password. You'll receive a confirmation email to verify your account.

items.question: I forgot my password. How can I reset it?
items.answer: Click on 'Forgot Password' on the login page. Enter your registered email address, and we'll send you instructions to reset your password securely.

items.question: How do I place an order?
items.answer: Browse our products, select the items you want, and click 'Add to Cart'. When you're ready, click the cart icon and proceed to checkout. Follow the prompts to enter your shipping and payment information.

items.question: Can I modify or cancel my order after placing it?
items.answer: You can modify or cancel your order within 1 hour of placing it. Go to 'My Orders' in your account, select the order, and choose the modify or cancel option. If it's been more than 1 hour, please contact our support team.

items.question: What payment methods do you accept?
items.answer: We accept all major credit and debit cards (Visa, MasterCard, American Express) and Apple Pay.

items.question: Are my payment details secure?
items.answer: Yes, we use industry-standard SSL encryption to protect your payment details. We do not store any credit card details.

items.question: How long does delivery usually take?
items.answer: Standard delivery takes 15–25 minutes, depending on the distance. Expedited delivery is available at checkout for prioritised delivery.

items.question: How can I track my order?
items.answer: Once your order ships, you'll receive a confirmation email with a tracking link. You can also track your order status in the 'My Orders' section of your account.

items.question: What is your return policy?
items.answer:
Non-Perishable Products
You may return your order within 14 days of delivery and return unused items within a further 14 days for a full refund. Items must be returned in their original condition.

If goods are faulty, not as described, or unfit for purpose, you are entitled to a full refund within 30 days of receipt.

Perishable Products
Due to the perishable nature of fresh goods, we do not accept returns or cancellations once they have been delivered.

If your order arrives damaged, incorrect, or unsafe to consume, you must notify us within 24 hours of delivery. Photo evidence may be required. Approved claims may be resolved with a replacement, store credit, or refund for the affected item(s).

items.question: The website is not loading properly. What should I do?
items.answer: Try clearing your browser cache and cookies, or accessing the site using an incognito window. If the problem persists, please contact our technical support.

items.question: I'm having trouble with the mobile app. Where can I get help?
items.answer: Make sure you have the latest version of the app installed. If issues continue, try reinstalling the app or contact our app support team with your device details.

items.question: How do I become a merchant on Urbandrop?
items.answer: To become a merchant, click on 'Sign Up as a Merchant' and provide your business details. Our team will review your application and guide you through the onboarding process.

items.question: What documents are required to register?
items.answer: You will need a valid business license, proof of ID, and bank account details for payouts. Specific requirements may vary by region.

items.question: How do I add products to my store?
items.answer: Log in to your Merchant Dashboard, navigate to the 'Products' secton, and click 'Add New Product'. Fill in the product details, upload images, and set your pricing and inventory levels.

items.question: Can I manage inventory levels automatically?
items.answer: Yes, our platform tracks your stock levels based on sales. You can also manually adjust inventory through the dashboard when you receive new shipments.

items.question: How am I notified of a new order?
items.answer: You will receive real-time notifications via email and the Merchant App. The order will also appear immediately in the 'Active Orders' tab of your dashboard.

items.question: Who handles the delivery?
items.answer: Urbandrop's network of delivery partners handles the fulfillment. Once an order is prepared, you request a pickup, and our partner delivers it to the customer.

items.question: When and how do I get paid?
items.answer: Payouts are processed weekly on Tuesdays for all completed orders from the previous week. Funds are directly deposited into the bank account you registered with.

items.question: What are the fees for selling on Urbandrop?
items.answer: We charge a commission on each successful sale. The exact percentage depends on your product category and subscription plan. There are no setup fees.

items.question: Who do I contact if I have a problem?
items.answer: Our dedicated Merchant Support team is available 24/7. You can reach us via live chat in your dashboard, email, or our priority phone line.

items.question: What if the Merchant Dashboard goes down?
items.answer: In the rare event of an outage, check our status page for updates. Our engineering team actively monitors system health to ensure maximum uptime.

hero.title: UrbanDrop
hero.subtitle: We are committed to protecting your privacy. This policy explains how we collect, use, share, and protect your personal data.

whoWeAre.title: 1. Who We Are
content: UrbanDrop is a food delivery platform operated by UrbanDrop Group Ltd, a company registered in the United Kingdom. We connect customers with local African, Caribbean, and Asian food vendors.

UrbanDrop ("we," "us," "our") is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, share, and protect your personal data when you use our platform. Please read it carefully. If you do not agree with our practices, please do not use our services.

lawfulBasis.title: 2. Lawful Basis for Processing
lawfulBasis.intro: We process your personal data on the following legal grounds under UK GDPR:
items: Contract performance — to provide the services you have requested, including order fulfilment and delivery.
Legal obligation — to comply with applicable laws and regulations.
Legitimate interests — to improve our platform, prevent fraud, and ensure security, where your rights do not override these interests.
Consent — for marketing communications and non-essential cookies. You may withdraw consent at any time.

infoCollect.title: 3. Information We Collect
infoCollect.intro: We collect the following personal data:
items: Personal details — name, email address, phone number, and delivery address.
Payment information — card details, billing address, and transaction history. Payment data is processed directly by our payment provider and is not stored by us.
Location data — real-time location, only when you use location-based features such as finding nearby vendors.
Device information — IP address, device type, operating system, app version, and browser type.
Usage data — how you interact with our app, including search history, order history, and customer support interactions.

howWeUse.title: 4. How We Use Your Information
howWeUse.intro: We use your personal data to:
items: Fulfil orders — process and deliver your food and grocery orders.
Provide customer support — respond to queries, complaints, and requests.
Personalise your experience — offer recommendations based on your preferences and order history.
Process payments — handle transactions securely.
Send marketing — with your consent, send promotional offers, updates, and surveys. You can opt out at any time.
Meet legal obligations — comply with tax regulations, fraud prevention requirements, and other legal duties.
Improve our service — analyse usage patterns to enhance the platform.

sharing.title: 5. Sharing Your Information
sharing.intro: We may share your personal data with:
items: Vendors — your name, order details, and delivery address are shared to fulfil your order.
Riders — your name, address, and contact number are shared for delivery. Riders lose access to this data once delivery is confirmed.
Payment processors — our payment partner is PCI-DSS certified and securely handles your payment data.
Service providers — trusted third parties who support our operations, including analytics, marketing, and customer service.
Authorities — we may disclose data where required by law, to protect our rights, or to prevent fraud.
Buyers or successors — in the event of a merger, acquisition, or asset sale, your data may transfer to the new entity. We will notify affected users.
sharing.note: We do not sell your personal data.

retention.title: 6. Data Retention
retention.intro: We keep personal data only for as long as necessary. Our standard retention periods are:
items: Order and transaction data — up to seven years, to comply with tax and accounting requirements.
Account information — until you delete your account, after which we delete or anonymise your data unless we are legally required to retain it.
Marketing preferences — until you withdraw consent.

rights.title: 7. Your Rights
rights.intro: Under UK data protection law, you have the right to:
items: Access — request a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
Correction — ask us to correct inaccurate or incomplete data.
Deletion — request that we delete your data, subject to legal obligations.
Restriction — ask us to limit how we process your data in certain circumstances.
Data portability — receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format.
Objection — object to processing based on legitimate interests, including direct marketing.
Withdraw consent — withdraw consent at any time where processing is consent-based.
rights.contact: To exercise any of these rights, contact us at support@urbandrop.io. We will respond within one month, in line with UK GDPR requirements.
rights.complaint: If you are not satisfied with how we handle your data, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) at ico.org.uk or by calling 0303 123 1113.

security.title: 8. Security
security.intro: We use industry-standard measures to protect your data, including:
items: Encryption — data is transmitted securely using SSL/TLS protocols.
Access controls — personal data is accessible only to authorised personnel.
Secure storage — data is held in protected environments.
security.note: No method of transmission or storage is completely secure. While we take all reasonable steps to protect your data, we cannot guarantee absolute security.

cookies.title: 9. Cookies and Tracking Technologies
cookies.intro: We use cookies and similar technologies to improve your experience. Cookies help us:
items: Remember your preferences.
Analyse how you use our platform.
Deliver relevant advertising.
cookies.consent: Where cookies are not strictly necessary, we will ask for your consent before placing them, in line with UK PECR rules. You can manage or disable cookies through your browser settings, though this may affect some functionality.

links.title: 10. Third-Party Links
links.content: Our platform may contain links to third-party websites. We are not responsible for the content or privacy practices of those sites. We encourage you to read their privacy policies before sharing any personal data.

children.title: 11. Children's Privacy
children.content: UrbanDrop is not intended for anyone under the age of 18. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. If we become aware that we have done so, we will delete it promptly.

international.title: 12. International Data Transfers
international.content: UrbanDrop operates within the UK and complies with UK GDPR. Where personal data is transferred outside the UK or EEA, we ensure appropriate safeguards are in place, such as Standard Contractual Clauses.

updates.title: 13. Changes to This Policy
updates.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we make significant changes, we will update the "Last Modified" date at the top and, where appropriate, notify you directly. Continued use of our platform after changes are posted constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

contact.title: 14. Contact Us
contact.intro: If you have any questions or concerns about this Privacy Policy, please contact us:

whatAreCookies.title: What Are Cookies?
whatAreCookies.desc: Cookies are small text files placed on your device when you visit a website or use an app. They help us remember your preferences, improve performance, and personalize your experience. Some cookies are essential for the Platform to work properly, while others help us understand how users interact with our services so we can improve them.

essential.title: Essential Cookies
essential.desc: These cookies are necessary for the Platform to function. They enable core features like secure login, checkout, and account management. Without them, certain services cannot be provided. Examples include keeping you logged in, managing your cart, and processing payments securely.

performance.title: Performance & Analytics Cookies
performance.desc: These cookies help us understand how visitors use UrbanDrop, such as which pages are most popular or where users encounter errors. We use this information to improve navigation, speed, and user experience. Examples include Google Analytics and app performance trackers. All data collected is aggregated and anonymous.

functionality.title: Functionality Cookies
functionality.desc: These cookies remember your preferences like language, location, or delivery address to make your experience easier.

advertising.title: Advertising & Marketing Cookies
advertising.desc: These cookies allow us and selected partners to show you relevant offers and promotions, both on UrbanDrop and on other websites or apps. They also help limit the number of times you see the same ad. Examples include Meta Pixel (Facebook/Instagram Ads) and Google Ads Remarketing. You can opt out of personalized ads via your account settings or browser privacy settings.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Cookies
thirdParty.desc: Some cookies on UrbanDrop come from trusted third parties that help us provide certain services, for example analytics providers (e.g., Google Analytics), advertising networks, and payment and delivery partners. These third parties may use cookies in accordance with their own privacy policies. UrbanDrop has no control over their cookie settings.

managing.title: Managing Cookies
managing.desc: You can manage or disable cookies via your browser or device settings. Most browsers let you see what cookies are stored on your device, delete existing cookies, and block some or all cookies. If you disable cookies, some parts of the Platform may not function properly (e.g., checkout or saved addresses). You can also adjust cookie preferences via the UrbanDrop Cookie Settings banner when you first visit our website or via your account settings.

consent.title: Consent
consent.desc: When you first visit UrbanDrop, you will see a banner asking for your consent to use non-essential cookies. By clicking "Accept All", you agree to the use of all cookies as described here. You can update your preferences at any time by clicking "Manage Cookies" in the site footer or via your profile. Essential cookies do not require consent as they are necessary to provide the service you requested.

updates.title: Updates to This Policy
updates.desc: We may update this Cookie Policy from time to time. The latest version will always be available on our website or app. If changes are significant, we will notify users by email or app notification before they take effect.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.desc: If you have any questions about our use of cookies, please contact:

notFoundPage.title: Page Not Found
notFoundPage.desc: The page you are looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.
notFoundPage.illustration: We couldn't find the page, but we found these fresh ingredients!
notFoundPage.searchSuggestion: Try searching for what you need or visit our Help Center.

banner.title: We value your privacy
banner.description: We use cookies to improve your experience. UrbanDrop uses essential cookies to make our site work, and optional cookies to help us understand how you use it, personalize content, and show you relevant offers. You can choose to accept all cookies, reject non-essential ones, or manage your preferences at any time.

modal.title: Cookie Preferences
modal.description: Manage how we use cookies on this site.

essential.title: Essential Cookies
essential.description: These cookies are needed for UrbanDrop to function properly. They enable secure login, checkout, and payment processing. You can not turn these off through our settings, but you can block them in your browser (which may affect functionality).
essential.examples: Examples: login sessions, shopping basket tracking, payment processing.

performance.title: Performance and Analytics Cookies
performance.description: These help us understand how visitors use UrbanDrop like which pages are most visited or where users experience delays. They allow us to improve speed, reliability, and overall usability.

functional.title: Functional Cookies
functional.description: These remember your preferences, such as language, location, and saved addresses, so you do not need to re-enter them each time.
functional.examples: Examples: preferred delivery address, saved shop filters.

advertising.title: Advertising and Marketing Cookies
advertising.description: We use these to show you personalized promotions and relevant ads, both on UrbanDrop and on other websites. They help us measure the performance of marketing campaigns and avoid showing the same ads repeatedly.
advertising.examples: Examples: Meta Pixel, Google Ads Remarketing.

footer.text1: You can update your cookie choices anytime by selecting "Manage Cookies" in our website footer or app menu.

intro.title: UrbanDrop Website Terms & Conditions
intro.p1: Welcome to UrbanDrop. These Terms and Conditions ("Terms") govern your access to and use of the UrbanDrop website, mobile application, and related services (collectively the "Platform") operated by UrbanDrop Group Limited ("UrbanDrop," "we," "us," or "our").
intro.p2: By accessing or using the Platform, you agree to these Terms. Please read them carefully before using the service. If you do not agree, you must not use the Platform.

about.title: 1. About UrbanDrop
content: UrbanDrop is an online marketplace that connects customers with independent merchants offering groceries, meals, and ethnic products for delivery or collection. We provide the digital infrastructure, payment processing, and customer interface, but we do not produce, own, or sell the items listed on the Platform.
When you place an order through UrbanDrop, you are purchasing directly from the merchant, not from UrbanDrop.

eligibility.title: 2. Eligibility
eligibility.content: You must be at least 18 years old to use the Platform. By using it, you confirm that:
items: You have the legal capacity to enter into binding agreements.
You will use the Platform only for lawful purposes.
You will provide accurate information when creating an account or placing an order.

creation.title: 3.1 Account Creation
creation.content: To place an order, you may need to create an UrbanDrop account. You must provide accurate details including your name, delivery address, and payment information.

security.title: 3.2 Account Security
security.content: You are responsible for keeping your login credentials secure. Any activity under your account is considered your responsibility.

misuse.title: 3.3 Misuse
misuse.content: UrbanDrop may suspend or close accounts used for fraudulent activities, abuse of promotions, or violations of these Terms.

placing.title: 4.1 Placing an Order
placing.content: When you place an order, you are offering to purchase products from a merchant listed on UrbanDrop. Once the merchant accepts your order, a contract is formed between you and that merchant.

confirmation.title: 4.2 Order Confirmation
confirmation.content: You will receive a confirmation via the Platform once your order is accepted. Merchants may cancel orders if items are unavailable or cannot be fulfilled, in which case no charge will be made.

payments.title: 4.3 Payments
payments.content: All payments are processed securely via UrbanDrop's payment partners. You authorize UrbanDrop to collect payments on behalf of the merchant.

pricing.title: 4.4 Pricing
pricing.content: Prices are set by merchants and include applicable taxes. Delivery, service, and packaging fees may apply and will be displayed before checkout.

areas.title: 5.1 Delivery Areas
areas.content: Delivery is available only in areas where UrbanDrop or its logistics partners operate.

partners.title: 5.2 Delivery Partners
partners.content: Orders may be delivered by UrbanDrop's delivery network or directly by the merchant. Delivery times are estimates and may vary due to traffic, weather, or other factors outside our control.

collection.title: 5.3 Collection Orders
collection.content: If you choose collection, please arrive at the scheduled time. Merchants may refuse to hold items longer than a reasonable period.

platform.title: 6.1 Platform Promotions
platform.content: UrbanDrop may occasionally offer promotions or discounts. Each has its own rules, which apply alongside these Terms.

merchant.title: 6.2 Merchant Promotions
merchant.content: Promotions created by merchants are solely funded by the merchant. UrbanDrop reviews and approves such offers but is not financially responsible for them.

misuse.title: 6.3 Misuse
misuse.content: UrbanDrop reserves the right to suspend or remove offers if abuse or fraudulent activity is detected.

merchant.title: 7. Merchant Responsibilities
merchant.content: Each merchant on UrbanDrop is an independent business. Merchants are solely responsible for:
items: The accuracy of product information, pricing, and images.
Food safety, hygiene, and proper product labelling.
Compliance with local licensing and trading laws.
merchant.note: UrbanDrop does not manufacture, test, or guarantee the products sold by merchants.

conduct.title: 8. Customer Conduct
items: Misuse the Platform or attempt to disrupt its operation.
Harass, abuse, or threaten merchants, drivers, or customer support.
Create multiple accounts to exploit promotions.
Post false reviews or misleading feedback.
conduct.note: UrbanDrop reserves the right to suspend or ban any account violating these standards.

before.title: 9.1 Before Merchant Acceptance
before.content: You can cancel an order free of charge at any time before it is accepted by the merchant.

after.title: 9.2 After Merchant Acceptance
after.content: Once a merchant has accepted your order, cancellation may not be possible, especially for perishable or prepared items. If the merchant has already started preparing your order, you will be charged the full amount.

merchant.title: 9.3 Cancellations by Merchant
merchant.content: Merchants may cancel an order if products are unavailable, the order cannot be fulfilled, or if delivery is not possible. In these cases, you will receive a full refund.

progress.title: 9.4 Delivery in Progress
progress.content: If your order is already on its way for delivery, it cannot be cancelled.

repeated.title: 9.5 Repeated Cancellations
repeated.content: Customers who repeatedly cancel orders after acceptance may have their account restricted or suspended.

general.title: 10.1 General Policy
general.content: As UrbanDrop connects customers directly with independent merchants, refunds and returns depend on the nature of the item ordered and the circumstances of the request.

perishable.title: 10.2 Perishable and Prepared Items
perishable.content: For perishable goods such as fresh produce, chilled or frozen foods, and cooked meals, refunds or returns are generally not available once an order has been delivered or collected, unless:
items: The item is damaged, expired, or unsafe for consumption at the time of delivery.
The wrong item was delivered.
The order was incomplete.
perishable.note: You must report these issues to UrbanDrop Support within 24 hours of delivery. Supporting evidence such as photos may be required.

nonPerishable.title: 10.3 Non-Perishable Items
nonPerishable.content: For packaged, sealed, or non-perishable items, merchants may accept returns in accordance with UK consumer law if:
items: The item is faulty or not as described.
It is returned unused and in its original packaging.

process.title: 10.4 Refund Process
process.content: Refunds are processed once the merchant confirms eligibility. UrbanDrop will credit the amount back to your original payment method within 5–10 business days of approval.

merchantResp.title: 10.5 Merchant Responsibility
merchantResp.content: Each merchant is responsible for handling refund requests related to their products. UrbanDrop facilitates communication and payment adjustments but is not the final decision-maker.

delivery.title: 10.6 Delivery Issues
delivery.content: If your order is delayed, lost, or delivered to the wrong address, please contact UrbanDrop Support immediately. Refunds or redelivery will be considered based on delivery tracking and merchant confirmation.

abuse.title: 10.7 Refund Abuse
abuse.content: Repeated refund claims without valid cause may result in your account being flagged or suspended.

ip.title: 11. Intellectual Property
ip.content: All trademarks, content, and technology on the Platform are owned by UrbanDrop Group Limited or its licensors. You may not copy, distribute, or modify any part of the Platform without written permission.

availability.title: 12. Platform Availability
availability.content: We aim to keep UrbanDrop available 24/7, but we may suspend or update services for maintenance. We are not liable for any temporary interruptions or data loss resulting from such events.

privacy.title: 13. Privacy and Data Protection
privacy.content: Your personal data is collected and processed in accordance with our Privacy Policy, available on the Platform. By using UrbanDrop, you consent to the use of your data for order processing, fraud prevention, and customer service.

role.title: 14.1 Role of UrbanDrop
role.content: UrbanDrop acts as an intermediary between customers and merchants. We are not responsible for:
items: Product quality, safety, or authenticity.
Incorrect or incomplete orders fulfilled by merchants.
Delivery issues caused by third parties.

indirect.title: 14.2 Indirect Losses
indirect.content: We are not liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from the use of the Platform.

maximum.title: 14.3 Maximum Liability
maximum.content: Our total liability to any user shall not exceed the amount paid for the relevant order or £50, whichever is lower.

termination.title: 15. Account Termination
termination.content: UrbanDrop may suspend or close your account at any time if you:
items: Violate these Terms.
Engage in fraudulent or abusive activities.
Misuse promotions or violate applicable laws.
termination.note: You may request account deletion at any time by contacting UrbanDrop Support.

links.title: 16. Third-Party Links
links.content: The Platform may contain links to third-party websites. UrbanDrop is not responsible for the content or policies of those sites, and accessing them is at your own risk.

changes.title: 17. Changes to these Terms
changes.content: UrbanDrop may update these Terms periodically. Changes will be effective upon posting on the Platform. Continued use of UrbanDrop constitutes acceptance of the latest version.

governing.title: 18. Governing Law
governing.content: These Terms are governed by the laws of England and Wales. Any disputes arising from or related to these Terms shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales.

contact.title: 19. Contact
contact.content: For questions, complaints, or refund requests, please contact:

customerTerms.title: Customer Terms & Conditions
customerTerms.intro: The terms that govern your use of the Urbandrop app and website, including orders, payments, subscriptions and recipe content.

readBeforeAccept.title: PLEASE READ BEFORE YOU ORDER
readBeforeAccept.content: These Terms form a legal contract between you and Urbandrop Group Limited. By creating an account, placing an order or subscribing to Urbandrop+, you accept these Terms. Your statutory rights under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 and the Consumer Contracts Regulations 2013 apply regardless of anything in this document and are not affected by it.

clause01.title: CLAUSE 01
clause01.subtitle: Who We Are & These Terms
clause01.content1: These Customer Terms & Conditions (the "Terms") form a contract between you (the "Customer", "you") and Urbandrop Group Limited ("Urbandrop", "we", "us", "our"), a private limited company registered in England & Wales with company number [00000000] and registered office at [Registered Office Address].
clause01.content2: These Terms apply when you visit urbandrop.io, use our mobile apps, create an account, place an order, subscribe to Urbandrop+, or view or interact with recipes on the Platform. By doing any of those things, you agree to be bound by these Terms.

whatUrbandropDoes.title: What Urbandrop does
whatUrbandropDoes.content: Urbandrop operates a multi-merchant marketplace and recipe platform specialising in African, Caribbean and Asian groceries and culturally relevant products. We connect you with independent merchants (the "Merchants") who list and sell their products, and we arrange last-mile delivery either ourselves or through third-party logistics partners. For most orders, Urbandrop acts as your agent in concluding the sale between you and the Merchant; the contract for the goods is with the Merchant, though we process payment and handle customer support on the Merchant's behalf. Where Urbandrop fulfils an order from one of its own micro-fulfilment centres, the contract for those goods is directly with Urbandrop.

platform.definition: The Urbandrop website (urbandrop.io), iOS and Android apps, recipe content, and any related services we operate.

account.definition: Your personal account on the Platform.

order.definition: A request you place through the Platform to purchase products from one or more Merchants.

merchant.definition: An independent retailer, supermarket or vendor listed on the Platform.

rider.definition: The delivery person who brings your Order from the Merchant to your delivery address, who may be engaged directly by us or through a delivery partner.

urbandropPlus.definition: Our paid subscription service described in Clause 10.

recipeContent.definition: Recipes, videos, images, and related content published on the Platform by us or by third-party creators.

statutoryRights.definition: Your rights as a consumer under UK law, including the Consumer Rights Act 2015 and the Consumer Contracts Regulations 2013, which apply regardless of these Terms.

whoCanCreate.title: 2.1 Who can create an account
whoCanCreate.content: You must be at least 18 years old to create an Account. If you are 16 or 17, you may use the Platform only under the supervision of a parent or guardian who is contractually responsible for your Orders.

accurateInfo.title: 2.2 Accurate information
accurateInfo.content: You must provide accurate, current and complete information when creating your Account and keep it up to date. You are responsible for the security of your login credentials. If you think someone else has used your Account, notify us immediately at support@urbandrop.io.

oneAccount.title: 2.3 One account per person
oneAccount.content: You may hold only one Account at a time. We may merge or close duplicate accounts at our discretion (any Urbandrop credit will be transferred to the surviving account).

personalUse.title: 2.4 Using the Platform for yourself
personalUse.content: You may use the Platform only for your own personal, non-commercial use. You may not resell products bought through the Platform, use an Account for another person's commercial purposes, or place orders using someone else's payment method without their permission.

whereWeDeliver.title: 3.1 Where we deliver
whereWeDeliver.content: Urbandrop is currently available in selected UK postcodes, starting in Bristol and expanding to Birmingham, London, Manchester and other UK cities over time. The app will tell you at checkout whether your address falls within an active service zone. We may add, change or remove service zones at any time.

whenWeDeliver.title: 3.2 When we deliver
whenWeDeliver.content: Delivery hours depend on the Merchant and your location and are shown in the app. Some Merchants and some products are only available at certain times.

stockAvailability.title: 3.3 Stock availability
stockAvailability.content: Product availability depends on the merchant's stock, which changes throughout the day. Occasionally, an item shown as available at checkout may be unavailable when the Merchant starts picking your Order. In that case, Clause 8 applies.

contractFormed.title: 4.1 How a contract is formed
contractFormed.content: When you place an Order through the Platform you are making an offer to buy the listed products. A contract is formed only when we (acting as the Merchant's agent, or for our own-fulfilment items, as seller) confirm that the Merchant has accepted your Order. Confirmation appears in the app and is typically sent as an email or push notification.

orderChanges.title: 4.2 Order changes
orderChanges.content: Once an Order has been accepted, you cannot change it through the app. You can contact support, but by that stage the Merchant may already be picking or have dispatched your Order, and we may not be able to change it.

minimumOrder.title: 4.3 Minimum order
minimumOrder.content: A minimum order value may apply. If your basket is below the Merchant's minimum, the app will show a small uplift fee or ask you to add more items. The current minimum and uplift fees are displayed at checkout.

substitutions.title: 4.4 Substitutions
substitutions.content: If an item is unavailable when the Merchant is picking your Order, the Merchant or we may offer a substitute. If a substitute is provided and you do not want it, you can reject it at the door or report it under Clause 8, and we will refund it.

rightToRefuse.title: 4.5 Right to refuse
rightToRefuse.content: We reserve the right to refuse or cancel an Order where, for example, the product is unavailable, the price was listed incorrectly due to an obvious error, we suspect fraudulent activity, the delivery address is outside our service zone, or the Order breaches these Terms or applicable law. If we do so, you will receive a full refund and we will tell you the reason.

productPrices.title: 5.1 Product prices
productPrices.content: Product prices are set by the Merchant (or by Urbandrop for our own-fulfilment items) and shown in the app in pounds sterling (GBP) inclusive of VAT where applicable. Prices may differ from in-store prices at the same Merchant and may change at any time before you place your Order.

fees.title: 5.2 Fees
fees.content: Typical fees shown at checkout include:
items: Delivery fee — varies by distance, time of day and demand. Urbandrop+ subscribers receive free or priority delivery on qualifying orders (see Clause 10).
Service fee — a percentage of your basket, charged on every Order, which contributes to running the Platform.
Small-basket uplift — a small fee applied when your basket falls below the minimum order threshold.
Tips — optional. 100% of any tip goes to the Rider; Urbandrop does not retain any portion.

surgePricing.title: 5.3 Surge and weather pricing
surgePricing.content: Delivery fees may increase during peak demand, adverse weather, or low-Rider-availability periods. The app will always show you the fee clearly before you confirm the Order.

payment.title: 5.4 Payment
payment.content: We accept major debit and credit cards, Apple Pay, Google Pay and any other payment methods shown at checkout. Payment is processed by our payment provider; we do not store full card details on our systems. By placing an Order you authorise us to charge your selected payment method for the full amount of the Order.

preAuth.title: 5.5 Pre-authorisation
preAuth.content: When you place an Order, we may place a temporary hold (pre-authorisation) on your payment method for the Order value. This is not a charge; the actual charge is taken when your Order is confirmed or dispatched. Pre-authorisation holds typically drop off within 3–7 working days depending on your bank.

receipts.title: 5.6 Receipts
receipts.content: A receipt for each Order is available in the app and, where you've chosen it, by email.

deliveryTime.title: 6.1 Delivery time estimate
deliveryTime.content: Delivery times shown in the app are estimates, not guarantees. Typical Orders arrive within 30–90 minutes, but actual times depend on Merchant preparation, Rider availability, traffic and weather.

whereWeDeliver.title: 6.2 Where we deliver
whereWeDeliver.content: We deliver to the address you select at checkout. You are responsible for making sure the address is correct and accessible. We cannot deliver to PO Boxes, lockers, or addresses where safe delivery is not reasonably possible. Where the building has restricted access, you must give clear instructions (buzzer, flat number, access code) in the delivery notes.

handover.title: 6.3 Handover
handover.content: Unless you have selected "leave at door" or another contact-free option, an adult must be available at the delivery address to receive the Order. For age-restricted items (Clause 7), contact-free delivery is not available.

ifNoOneAvailable.title: 6.4 If no one is available
ifNoOneAvailable.content: If the Rider cannot reach you on arrival (no answer at the door, no response to app or phone contact), they will wait a reasonable time (typically 5 minutes) before reporting the Order as undeliverable. In that case:
items: perishable items (fresh meat, dairy, fresh produce, frozen items) may be safely disposed of to prevent food-safety risk, and are not refundable beyond what your statutory rights require;
non-perishable items may be returned to the Merchant or held for re-delivery if feasible, at our discretion and potentially for an additional fee;
the delivery fee is not refundable where the fault is not ours.

leaveAtDoor.title: 6.5 Leave-at-door
leaveAtDoor.content: If you select leave-at-door or give consent to leave the Order in a specified safe place, risk in the goods passes to you once the Rider has left the Order there and captured the confirmation photo. We are not responsible for theft or tampering after that point.

riskOwnership.title: 6.6 Risk and ownership
riskOwnership.content: Risk in the goods passes to you on handover (or on leave-at-door placement under 6.5). Ownership passes to you once full payment has been received and the goods have been handed over.

clause07.title: CLAUSE 07
clause07.subtitle: Age-Restricted Products
clause07.intro: Certain products sold on the Platform are restricted by law in the UK, including alcohol, tobacco products, nicotine-vaping products, knives and bladed products, and certain solvents. We comply strictly with the Licensing Act 2003, the Tobacco and Related Products Regulations 2016, the Offensive Weapons Act 2019 and all related legislation.

minimumAge.title: 7.1 Minimum age
minimumAge.content: You must be at least 18 years old to order alcohol, tobacco, vapes or knives through the Platform. By placing an Order for these items you confirm that you are 18 or over.

challenge25.title: 7.2 Challenge 25 on delivery
challenge25.content: We operate a Challenge 25 policy. If the person receiving the Order appears to be under 25, the Rider will ask for photo ID (UK passport, UK photocard driving licence, or Home Office-issued identity document). The Rider is required by law to refuse to hand over the restricted items if ID cannot be produced or if the recipient appears intoxicated.

idFails.title: 7.3 What happens if ID fails
idFails.content: If the restricted items are refused at the door, those items will be returned to the Merchant and your payment method refunded for those items. The rest of the Order will be delivered as normal. The delivery fee is not refundable where delivery was attempted in good faith.

proxyPurchasing.title: 7.4 Proxy purchasing
proxyPurchasing.content: It is a criminal offence to buy alcohol, tobacco or similar restricted items on behalf of a person under 18. If we have reason to believe your Order is a proxy purchase, we will refuse or cancel it, may suspend or terminate your Account, and may report the matter to the police and relevant licensing authority.

clause08.title: CLAUSE 08
clause08.subtitle: Missing, Damaged or Wrong Items
clause08.intro: We want you to be happy with every Order. If something isn't right, here's how we typically resolve it — alongside your statutory rights which always apply.

rows.issue: Missing item from a delivered order
rows.resolution: Refund for the missing item (to original payment method) or Urbandrop credit — your choice
Refund or replacement on your next order, at your choice
Refund for the wrong item; you keep or dispose of it
Full refund (including delivery fee) or redelivery

rows.issue: Quality not as expected (e.g., off taste, poor appearance)
rows.resolution: Case-by-case goodwill resolution; statutory rights apply under Consumer Rights Act 2015

rows.issue: Order cancelled by you after placement
rows.resolution: See Clause 9 — varies by order stage
rows.timeframe: Via the app before Merchant accepts

rows.issue: Order cancelled by Merchant or Urbandrop
rows.resolution: Full refund automatically; no action needed

howToReport.title: 8.2 How to report an issue
howToReport.content: Use the "Help with this Order" option in the app, or email support@urbandrop.io. Have the Order number, a description of the issue, and photos where relevant. We aim to respond within two (2) working days and resolve within five (5) working days, and usually much faster for straightforward cases.

whenRefundsIssued.title: 8.3 When refunds are issued
whenRefundsIssued.content: Where a refund is agreed, it will be made to your original payment method and typically arrives within 3–10 working days depending on your bank. For Urbandrop credit, it is added to your Account immediately and appears on your next Order.

whenWeDecline.title: 8.4 When we may decline a refund
whenWeDecline.content: We may decline a refund request where:
items: the issue is reported outside the time windows above without good reason;
the item was clearly consumed in full before the issue was raised without a reasonable explanation;
we have reasonable evidence the claim is not genuine (for example, repeated identical claims across multiple Orders);
the issue results from your own act or omission (for example, selecting leave-at-door in a location where theft was foreseeable).
whenWeDecline.note: In those cases we will explain our reasoning and you may escalate under Clause 18.

statutoryRights.title: 8.5 Your statutory rights
statutoryRights.content: Nothing in this Clause limits your rights under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 (goods must be of satisfactory quality, fit for purpose, and as described) or the Consumer Contracts Regulations 2013. Those rights are in addition to the resolutions above.

fraudulentClaims.title: FRAUDULENT CLAIMS
fraudulentClaims.content: We take refund claims in good faith. Submitting knowingly false or dishonest claims is fraud under the Fraud Act 2006, will result in Account termination, and we reserve the right to pursue recovery of wrongfully-refunded sums and report the matter to the police.

beforeAcceptance.title: 9.1 Cancelling before Merchant acceptance
beforeAcceptance.content: You can cancel an Order at any time before the Merchant accepts it, through the app. No cancellation fee applies, and you will receive a full refund.

afterAcceptance.title: 9.2 Cancelling after acceptance
afterAcceptance.content: Once the Merchant has accepted and begun to prepare or pick your Order, cancellation may incur a fee to reflect the costs already incurred:
items: Accepted but not yet picked — typically a small cancellation fee covering Merchant and delivery-dispatch costs.
Picked but not yet out for delivery — typically a cancellation fee equal to the product cost plus the delivery fee, as perishable and picked goods cannot easily be returned to the shelf.
Out for delivery or delivered — cancellation is not normally possible. You may raise an issue under Clause 8 if appropriate.
afterAcceptance.note: The app will show you the applicable cancellation fee (if any) before you confirm cancellation.

rightToCancel.title: 9.3 Right to cancel under the Consumer Contracts Regulations 2013
rightToCancel.content: The Consumer Contracts Regulations 2013 give consumers a 14-day "cooling-off" period for many online purchases. However, Regulation 28 excludes the cooling-off period for contracts to supply goods liable to deteriorate or expire rapidly (most fresh food and groceries) and sealed goods which are not suitable for return due to health protection or hygiene reasons once unsealed. Because of the nature of our products, the cooling-off period will not typically apply. This does not affect your statutory rights under the Consumer Rights Act 2015.

cancellationByUs.title: 9.4 Cancellation by us
cancellationByUs.content: We may cancel an Order under Clause 4.5. Where we do, we refund in full and (where the cancellation was not caused by you) will usually offer a credit or goodwill gesture by way of apology.

clause10.title: CLAUSE 10
clause10.subtitle: Urbandrop+ Subscription
clause10.intro: Urbandrop+ is our optional paid subscription. This Clause sets out the terms specific to Urbandrop+. All other Clauses of these Terms continue to apply to your Urbandrop+ Orders.

whatYouGet.title: 10.1 What you get
whatYouGet.content: Urbandrop+ benefits currently include (we may update them from time to time with notice):
items: free standard delivery on qualifying Orders above the stated threshold;
priority delivery slots where available;
exclusive member-only deals and promotional pricing;
ad-free recipe browsing.
whatYouGet.note: Benefits that are subject to third-party availability (for example, priority slots during very high demand) are offered on a reasonable-endeavours basis.

pricing.title: 10.2 Pricing
pricing.content: Urbandrop+ is offered on a monthly plan and an annual plan. Prices are shown in the app and include VAT where applicable. We may change future pricing; existing subscribers will be notified at least 30 days before a price change takes effect, and you may cancel if you do not wish to continue at the new price.

freeTrials.title: 10.3 Free trials
freeTrials.content: If you start Urbandrop+ with a free trial, we will tell you at sign-up how long the trial lasts and the price that will apply when it ends. You can cancel before the trial ends, and you will not be charged. If you do not cancel, you authorise us to charge your payment method for the subscription fee when the trial ends, and then at each renewal.

autoRenewal.title: 10.4 Auto-renewal
autoRenewal.content: Urbandrop+ renews automatically at the end of each billing period (monthly or annual) unless you cancel. We will charge your selected payment method on the renewal date. This auto-renewal is lawful only because we tell you about it clearly here and in the app. You can turn off auto-renewal at any time in your Account settings.

cancelling.title: 10.5 Cancelling Urbandrop+
cancelling.content: You can cancel at any time through your Account settings or by contacting support. Cancellation stops future renewals but does not entitle you to a refund of the current billing period, except where:
items: Under the Consumer Contracts Regulations 2013, within 14 days of first signing up, you have a 14-day cooling-off period. If you cancel within 14 days, we will refund the subscription fee in full, less a proportional deduction for any use of paid benefits (for example, delivery fees saved).
After 14 days, cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period. Benefits continue until that date.

changesToBenefits.title: 10.7 Changes to benefits
changesToBenefits.content: We may add, modify or remove Urbandrop+ benefits. Where a change materially reduces the overall value of the subscription, we will notify you at least 30 days in advance, and you may cancel for a pro rata refund of the unused portion of your subscription.

terminationByUs.title: 10.8 Termination by us
terminationByUs.content: We may terminate your Urbandrop+ subscription and close your Account for material breach of these Terms, fraudulent activity, or abusive use of benefits. If we do so for cause, we are not obliged to refund any unused portion. If we terminate without cause, we will refund a pro-rata amount for the unused period.

recipePersonalUse.title: 11.1 Recipe Content is for personal use
recipePersonalUse.content: You may view and cook from Recipe Content for your own personal, non-commercial use. Except for what the Platform's share buttons allow (e.g., sharing a link to a recipe on social media), you may not copy, reproduce, republish, or distribute Recipe Content.

accuracyOfRecipes.title: 11.2 Accuracy of recipes
accuracyOfRecipes.content: Recipes are provided for general information only. Whilst our Creators and we take reasonable care, we do not guarantee that every recipe is error-free or suitable for every individual, particularly where allergies, intolerances, pregnancy, medical conditions or dietary restrictions are concerned. Always check ingredient labels and consult a qualified professional where appropriate.

recipeToBasket.title: 11.3 Recipe-to-basket
recipeToBasket.content: Many recipes include a "Shop the recipe" button that adds suggested ingredients to your basket from available Merchants. Suggested quantities are estimates — check your basket before checkout. Availability, price and Merchant assignment are as shown at checkout.

userContent.title: 11.4 Your User Content
userContent.content: If you submit a rating, review, comment, photo or other User Content:
items: You must own it or have the right to submit it;

you must not submit content that is unlawful, defamatory, obscene, threatening, harassing, discriminatory, misleading, or that infringes anyone else's rights (including privacy and intellectual property);

You grant Urbandrop a worldwide, royalty-free, non-exclusive, transferable, sub-licensable licence to use, display, reproduce, adapt, translate and distribute your User Content for the purpose of operating and promoting the Platform. You keep ownership of your User Content;
You waive any "moral rights" you may have in the User Content under the Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 to the extent permitted by law;
we may remove, edit or refuse to publish User Content at our discretion, particularly where it breaches these Terms or we receive a valid takedown request.

reviewsMerchants.title: 11.5 Reviews of Merchants and Products
reviewsMerchants.content: We welcome honest, lawful reviews. Reviews must be based on genuine experience. Fake, incentivised or coordinated reviews may breach the Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Act 2024 and will be removed.

creatorContent.title: 11.6 Creator content
creatorContent.content: Recipe Content produced by Creators is published under a separate Creator Agreement between Urbandrop and the Creator. For Platform users, the terms above apply. If you believe any Recipe Content infringes your rights, contact legal@urbandrop.io and we will investigate and act in line with UK law.

allergenInfo.title: 12.1 Allergen information
allergenInfo.content: Where applicable, allergen information for each product is provided by the Merchant or manufacturer and displayed on the product listing. This information is subject to the Food Information Regulations 2014 and covers the fourteen major allergens listed in Annex II of Regulation (EU) No 1169/2011 (as retained in UK law).

alwaysCheckPackaging.title: 12.2 Always check packaging
alwaysCheckPackaging.content: Product information, including ingredient lists, allergens and nutritional values, can change without notice. Always check the packaging on delivery before you consume anything, especially if you have an allergy or intolerance. If there is any discrepancy between information in the app and the packaging, rely on the packaging.

crossContamination.title: 12.3 Cross-contamination
crossContamination.content: Some Merchants operate premises that handle multiple allergens. We cannot guarantee that any product is free from trace amounts of allergens it does not declare.

temperatureFoodSafety.title: 12.4 Temperature and food safety
temperatureFoodSafety.content: On delivery, refrigerate or freeze temperature-sensitive items promptly. Do not consume visibly spoiled items, past their use-by date, or damaged. Raise any food-safety concerns with us immediately through the in-app help channel.

seriousAllergies.title: SERIOUS ALLERGIES
seriousAllergies.content: If you have a serious or life-threatening allergy, please take extra care. We strongly recommend contacting the Merchant directly (contact details available through the Platform) before you order if you have any doubt about an ingredient or product handling.

clause13.title: CLAUSE 13
clause13.subtitle: Promotions & Vouchers
clause13.content: We may offer promotional codes, introductory offers, referral rewards, and Urbandrop credit from time to time. Unless stated otherwise in the specific offer terms:
items: Promotions are one per customer, per household, per payment method.
they are non-transferable and have no cash value;
they may be used only once unless the specific terms say otherwise;
they cannot be combined with other promotions;
they expire on the date stated — Urbandrop credit typically expires 12 months from date of issue;
we may withdraw or modify an offer at any time where it is being abused or where we have an objectively justifiable reason.
clause13.note: If a promotion has additional specific terms, those specific terms take precedence over this Clause in the event of a conflict.

clause14.title: CLAUSE 14
clause14.subtitle: Acceptable Use
items: use the Platform for any unlawful purpose or in a way that harms others;
create Accounts using false details, impersonation, or automated scripts;
use the Platform to resell products, run a commercial operation, or in a way that exceeds normal personal use;
reverse-engineer, decompile, scrape or extract data from the Platform except through published APIs with our written consent;
introduce viruses, malware, or anything designed to disrupt the Platform;
abuse, harass, threaten or discriminate against Riders, Merchant staff, Creators, other users or Urbandrop staff;
abuse refund, promotional or complaint processes by making knowingly false claims;

access the Platform using a VPN, location-spoofer or other tool designed to hide your true location, where doing so is used to obtain pricing or availability to which you would not otherwise be entitled.
clause14.note: Breach of this Clause may result in suspension or termination of your Account and, where appropriate, legal action or referral to the police.

whatWeAreResponsible.title: 15.1 What we are responsible for
whatWeAreResponsible.content: We are responsible for losses that you suffer as a foreseeable result of our breaking this contract or our failure to use reasonable care and skill, but we are not responsible for any loss or damage that is not foreseeable. A loss is foreseeable if it is either obvious that it will happen, or if, at the time you accepted these Terms, both we and you knew it might happen.

whatWeAreNotResponsible.title: 15.2 What we are not responsible for
whatWeAreNotResponsible.content: Subject always to Clause 15.3 and your statutory rights, we are not liable for:
items: indirect or consequential loss, loss of profit, loss of business, loss of opportunity, or loss of goodwill;
losses caused by events outside our reasonable control (see Clause 21.7 — Force majeure);
the acts or omissions of Merchants beyond what is reasonably attributable to us as their agent;
loss resulting from your failure to keep your Account credentials secure;
loss resulting from reliance on Recipe Content where you have not checked the underlying product packaging (see Clause 12.2).

liabilityWeDoNotExclude.title: 15.3 Liability we do not exclude
liabilityWeDoNotExclude.content: Nothing in these Terms limits or excludes our liability for:
items: death or personal injury caused by our negligence;
fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation;
defective products under the Consumer Protection Act 1987;
breach of your statutory rights as a consumer under the Consumer Rights Act 2015;
any other liability which cannot lawfully be excluded or limited.

capOnLiability.title: 15.4 Cap on liability
capOnLiability.content: Where liability can lawfully be limited, our total liability to you in respect of any single Order, and in aggregate for related events in any 12-month period, is limited to the greater of £100 or the value of the Orders placed in that period. This cap does not apply to the matters listed in Clause 15.3.

clause16.title: CLAUSE 16
clause16.subtitle: Data Protection & Privacy
clause16.content1: Urbandrop is the controller of the Personal Data we collect about you. We process Personal Data in accordance with the UK GDPR, the Data Protection Act 2018, and the Privacy and Electronic Communications Regulations 2003.
clause16.content2: Our full Privacy Policy explains what Personal Data we collect, why we collect it, how we use it, how long we keep it, who we share it with (including Merchants, Riders, payment providers, and identity-verification partners), and how you can exercise your rights. It is available at urbandrop.io/privacy and forms part of these Terms.
clause16.content3: Your data-subject rights under UK GDPR include access, rectification, erasure (subject to legal retention obligations), restriction, portability and objection. To exercise any of these, email dpo@urbandrop.io. You may also complain to the Information Commissioner's Office (ico.org.uk).
clause16.icoRegistration: Urbandrop is registered with the ICO under registration number [ZA000000].

clause17.title: CLAUSE 17
clause17.subtitle: Intellectual Property
clause17.content: The Platform, the Urbandrop name, the Urbandrop logo, the "Where tradition meets convenience" tagline, the rider pin-leaf mark, the app user interface, Recipe Content produced by or for us, and all associated designs, code and data are owned by Urbandrop or our licensors and are protected by copyright, trade-mark, and database-right laws of England & Wales and internationally. You are granted a limited, personal, revocable, non-transferable licence to use the Platform for its intended purpose as a Customer.
clause17.merchantBrands: Merchants' brands, logos and product imagery are the intellectual property of the respective Merchants or brand owners. You may not use them except as the Platform permits.

raiseWithUs.title: 18.1 Raise it with us first
raiseWithUs.content: If you have a complaint — about a Merchant, a Rider, an Order, a refund decision, a subscription matter, or anything else — please contact us first through the in-app "Help" channel or at support@urbandrop.io. We aim to acknowledge within two (2) working days and substantively respond within ten (10) working days.

escalation.title: 18.2 Escalation
escalation.content: If you are not satisfied with our response, you may escalate in writing to complaints@urbandrop.io for review by a manager not involved in the original decision.

alternativeDispute.title: 18.3 Alternative dispute resolution
alternativeDispute.content: Under the Alternative Dispute Resolution for Consumer Disputes (Competent Authorities and Information) Regulations 2015, we are not obliged to use, and do not currently use, an ADR provider. However we are happy to consider any reasonable ADR proposal on a case-by-case basis.

rightToCourt.title: 18.4 Your right to go to court
rightToCourt.content: Nothing in these Terms prevents you from bringing a claim in the courts or from contacting relevant consumer, trading standards, licensing or data-protection regulators.

clause19.title: CLAUSE 19
clause19.subtitle: Changes to these Terms
clause19.content1: We may update these Terms from time to time, for example to reflect changes in the law, new features, or to improve clarity. We will post the updated Terms on the Platform with a new effective date at the top of this document.
clause19.content2: Where a change materially affects your rights (for example, a change to cancellation rights, refund policy, or Urbandrop+ pricing), we will give you at least 30 days' advance notice by email or in-app notification. If you do not accept the change, you may close your Account or cancel your Urbandrop+ subscription before the effective date. Continuing to use the Platform after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms.
clause19.content3: Minor, non-material changes (for example, clarifying wording, updating contact details) take effect when posted.

byYou.title: 20.1 By you
byYou.content: You may close your Account at any time through Account settings or by contacting support. Any unshipped Orders will be cancelled per Clause 9; any active Urbandrop+ subscription will be handled per Clause 10.5.

byUs.title: 20.2 By us
byUs.content: We may suspend or terminate your Account where:
items: you have materially breached these Terms;
we reasonably suspect fraudulent, abusive, or unlawful use of the Platform;
we are required to do so by law or by a regulator;
you have not used your Account for a prolonged period and it is dormant.
byUs.note: Where we terminate for a reason not involving your fault, we will refund any Urbandrop+ unused period on a pro-rata basis and any Urbandrop credit not yet expired. Where we terminate for material breach by you, we are not obliged to refund.

effect.title: 20.3 Effect
effect.content: On termination, your right to use the Platform ends. Clauses that by their nature survive — including 11 (User Content licence), 15 (Liability), 16 (Data), 17 (IP), 18 (Complaints), and 21 (General) — continue to apply.

entireAgreement.title: 21.1 Entire agreement
entireAgreement.content: These Terms, together with the Privacy Policy, any Urbandrop+ Subscription terms you accept, and any specific offer terms, form the entire agreement between you and us. They replace any prior arrangements.

transferContract.title: 21.2 If we have to transfer our contract with you
transferContract.content: We may transfer our rights and obligations under these Terms to another organisation, for example in the context of a group reorganisation, sale, merger, or investment. We will tell you in writing if this happens, and we will ensure the transfer does not reduce your rights. You may not transfer your rights or obligations without our written consent.

noWaiver.title: 21.3 No waiver
noWaiver.content: If we do not immediately insist on you doing anything you are required to do under these Terms, or delay in taking action against you in respect of your breaking this contract, that will not mean that you do not have to do those things or prevent us taking steps against you later.

severability.title: 21.4 Severability
severability.content: Each of the clauses of these Terms operates separately. If any court or relevant authority decides that any of them is unlawful, the remaining clauses remain in full force and effect.

thirdParties.title: 21.5 Third parties
thirdParties.content: These Terms are between you and us. No other person has any right to enforce any of them under the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999.

notices.title: 21.6 Notices
notices.content: We may send you notices via the app, by email to the address on your Account, or by SMS to your verified mobile number. You may send us notices by email to legal@urbandrop.io or by post to our registered office.

forceMajeure.title: 21.7 Force majeure
forceMajeure.content: Neither party is liable for failure or delay in performing these Terms due to events beyond reasonable control — including natural disasters, war, terrorism, epidemic, strikes, transport disruption, government action, or infrastructure outage — provided the affected party takes reasonable steps to mitigate. Your statutory rights under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 and Consumer Contracts Regulations 2013 remain unaffected.

governingLaw.title: 21.8 Governing law and jurisdiction
governingLaw.content: These Terms are governed by the laws of England and Wales. Any dispute or claim arising out of or in connection with these Terms will be subject to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales, save that if you reside in Scotland or Northern Ireland you may also bring proceedings in your local courts.

merchantTerms.title: Merchant Terms & Conditions
merchantTerms.intro: These Merchant Terms and Conditions govern the relationship between UrbanDrop Group Limited and the merchant entity or individual that registers to sell goods via the UrbanDrop platform.

roleOfUrbanDrop.title: 1. Role of UrbanDrop
roleOfUrbanDrop.content: UrbanDrop operates an online marketplace that enables Merchants to offer goods to customers and facilitates order placement, payment processing, and customer support. UrbanDrop does not manufacture, prepare, store, or supply food or grocery items.
roleOfUrbanDrop.p2: All contracts for the sale of goods are formed directly between the Merchant and the customer. The Merchant is the seller of record for all orders fulfilled through the Platform.

eligibility.title: 2. Merchant Eligibility and Onboarding
eligibility.content: By registering as a Merchant, you confirm that:
items: You are legally established and authorised to trade in the United Kingdom
You hold all required licences, permits, and approvals to sell food or grocery products
You comply with all applicable food safety, hygiene, trading standards, and consumer protection laws
All information provided during onboarding is accurate and kept up to date
eligibility.note: UrbanDrop reserves the right to reject, suspend, or terminate Merchant accounts that fail to meet these requirements.
eligibility.acceptance: You agree that your electronic acceptance of these Terms constitutes a legally binding agreement between you and UrbanDrop. UrbanDrop maintains electronic records of Merchant acceptance including the date, timestamp, IP address, and version of Terms at acceptance.

obligations.title: 3. Merchant Obligations
obligations.content: The Merchant is solely responsible for:
items: The quality, safety, legality, and accuracy of all products listed
Correct preparation, packaging, and labelling of goods
Compliance with UK food safety legislation, including allergen and ingredient disclosure
Ensuring prices, descriptions, images, and availability are accurate at all times
Preparing orders promptly and in accordance with customer instructions
Ensuring goods are suitable for delivery and consumption
obligations.note: The Merchant must not substitute items without customer approval unless explicitly permitted by Platform functionality.

orders.title: 4. Orders and Fulfilment
items: Accept and fulfil orders in good faith
Avoid unnecessary cancellations
Notify UrbanDrop immediately of stock shortages, delays, or fulfilment issues
orders.note: UrbanDrop may monitor order acceptance rates, cancellation rates, and fulfilment performance. Repeated failures may result in penalties, reduced visibility, suspension, or termination.

pricing.title: 5. Pricing and Promotions
pricing.content: Merchants set their own prices but acknowledge that:
items: Prices must be fair, transparent, and compliant with consumer protection law
UrbanDrop may display prices inclusive of service and delivery fees
UrbanDrop may offer promotions, discounts, or credits at its own cost unless otherwise agreed
Merchants may not inflate prices in a way that misleads customers or breaches applicable law

payments.title: 6. Payments and Settlement
payments.content1: UrbanDrop collects payments from customers on behalf of Merchants and acts as a commercial agent for payment processing purposes.
payments.content2: Merchants are entitled to receive the net value of completed orders, calculated as:
items: Total order value minus Commission fees, Delivery fees, Service fees
Refunds, chargebacks, or adjustments
Any other agreed charges
payments.settlement: Settlement is made according to the agreed payout cycle. UrbanDrop may withhold or offset amounts where refunds, disputes, or suspected breaches exist.
payments.stripeContent: UrbanDrop uses Stripe to provide payment processing services. By agreeing to these Terms, you agree to be bound by the Stripe Services Agreement. You authorise UrbanDrop to share accurate business information with Stripe for onboarding, compliance, fraud prevention, and payment processing.

refunds.title: 7. Refunds, Complaints, and Chargebacks
refunds.content: UrbanDrop may issue refunds to customers in accordance with its refund policy. Where a refund or chargeback arises due to:
items: Incorrect items
Missing items
Poor food quality
Safety or hygiene issues
Merchant cancellation or failure to deliver
refunds.note: The Merchant bears full financial responsibility. UrbanDrop may deduct refunded amounts from future payouts or invoice the Merchant directly.

foodSafety.title: 8. Food Safety and Compliance
foodSafety.content1: Merchants must comply with all UK food safety and hygiene regulations, allergen labelling requirements, and product traceability obligations.
foodSafety.content2: Merchants must immediately notify UrbanDrop of:
items: Any food safety incident
Any regulatory investigation or enforcement action
Any product recall or contamination risk
foodSafety.note: Failure to do so is grounds for immediate termination.

products.title: 9. Permitted Products and Prohibited Items
products.permittedContent: Merchants may only list and sell food and grocery products that are lawful to sell in the United Kingdom and expressly permitted by UrbanDrop through the Platform.
products.prohibitedContent: The following products are strictly prohibited:
items: Alcoholic beverages of any kind
Tobacco products, including cigarettes, cigars, rolling tobacco, and vaping products
Illegal drugs, controlled substances, and psychoactive substances
Prescription-only medicines or medical products requiring authorisation
Any other products prohibited by applicable law or UrbanDrop policies
products.warning: Any attempt to list or sell alcohol, tobacco, or prohibited substances is a material breach of these Terms.

delivery.title: 10. Delivery
delivery.content: Where delivery is facilitated via the Platform, the Merchant remains responsible for:
items: Proper packaging
Temperature control
Food integrity at the point of handover
delivery.note: UrbanDrop is not responsible for the quality of goods after preparation but before delivery, except where required by law.

insurance.title: 11. Insurance and Indemnity
insurance.content1: Merchants must maintain adequate insurance, including public liability and product liability insurance.
insurance.content2: The Merchant agrees to indemnify and hold harmless UrbanDrop against all claims, losses, damages, fines, penalties, and legal costs arising from:
items: Food safety breaches
Consumer claims relating to goods
Regulatory non-compliance
Merchant negligence or misconduct

conduct.title: 12. Platform Use and Conduct
items: Manipulate ratings or reviews
Engage in fraudulent activity
Circumvent the Platform to avoid fees
Use customer data for unauthorised purposes
conduct.note: UrbanDrop may suspend or terminate access for any breach.

suspension.title: 13. Suspension and Termination
suspension.content: UrbanDrop may suspend or terminate the Merchant account immediately where:
items: There is a serious breach of these Terms
Food safety is compromised
Fraud or abuse is suspected
Regulatory action is taken against the Merchant
suspension.note: Merchants may terminate with notice, subject to outstanding obligations. Termination does not affect accrued rights, liabilities, or indemnities.

liability.title: 14. Limitation of Liability
liability.content: To the maximum extent permitted by law:
items: UrbanDrop is not liable for loss of profits, revenue, or business
UrbanDrop does not guarantee order volume or earnings
liability.note: Nothing in these Terms limits liability where such limitation is unlawful.

dataProtection.title: 15. Data Protection
dataProtection.content: Merchants must process customer data in accordance with the UK GDPR and only for order fulfilment. Use of customer data for marketing or unrelated activities is strictly prohibited.

amendments.title: 16. Amendments
amendments.content: UrbanDrop may update these Terms from time to time. Continued use of the Platform constitutes acceptance of updated Terms.

governingLaw.title: 17. Governing Law and Jurisdiction
governingLaw.content: The laws of England and Wales govern these Terms. The courts of England and Wales have exclusive jurisdiction.

promotionsTerms.title: Promotions & Discounts
promotionsTerms.intro: Rules surrounding the use of promo codes, vouchers, and special discount events.

application.title: Application of Codes
application.content: Promo codes must be entered at checkout. They cannot be applied retrospectively to orders already placed.

limitations.title: Limitations
limitations.content: Only one promo code can be used per transaction unless explicitly stated otherwise. Promos hold no cash value.

fairUsage.title: Fair Usage
fairUsage.content: Creation of multiple accounts to exploit new-user promotions is strictly prohibited and will result in account banning.

modification.title: Modification
modification.content: Urbandrop reserves the right to modify, cancel, or suspend promotions at any time without prior notice.

riderTerms.title: Rider Terms & Conditions
riderTerms.intro: The terms under which you provide last-mile delivery services connected to the Urbandrop platform across the United Kingdom.

readBeforeAccept.title: Read This Before You Accept
readBeforeAccept.content: This is a legally binding agreement. By creating a Rider Account or accepting a delivery through Urbandrop, you confirm you have read, understood, and agree to these terms. You are free to seek independent legal advice, and we encourage you to do so. If you do not agree with any part of these terms, do not use the platform.

parties.title: 01 Parties & Definitions
parties.content: These Rider Terms & Conditions (the "Terms") form a binding agreement between Urbandrop Group Limited, a private limited company incorporated in England & Wales with its registered office at [Bristol, United Kingdom], company number [16026087] ("Urbandrop", "we", "us", "our"), and the individual or entity performing last-mile delivery services in connection with the Urbandrop platform ("Rider", "you", "your").

platform.definition: The Urbandrop mobile applications, rider app, merchant portal, admin systems and any related websites, APIs or services operated by Urbandrop.

riderAccount.definition: Your personal account on the Platform granting access to delivery opportunities.

delivery.definition: A single order of groceries or goods collected from a merchant and delivered to a customer via the Platform.

fleetPartner.definition: A third-party logistics provider with which Urbandrop has contracted for delivery services (e.g., Stuart, Gophr, Relay or equivalent).

directRider.definition: A Rider who contracts directly with Urbandrop as a self-employed independent contractor.

fleetRider.definition: A Rider engaged by a Fleet Partner who fulfils Urbandrop Deliveries under the Fleet Partner's own agreement.

customer.definition: The end-user placing an order through the Platform.

merchant.definition: A retailer, supermarket, or vendor listed on the Platform.

personalData.definition: As defined in the UK General Data Protection Regulation (UK GDPR) and the Data Protection Act 2018.

relationship.title: 02 Nature of the Relationship
relationship.content: Urbandrop operates a hybrid delivery model. Depending on how you access Deliveries, you are either a Direct Rider (contracting with Urbandrop) or a Fleet Rider (engaged by a Fleet Partner and fulfilling Urbandrop Deliveries under a separate agreement with that Fleet Partner). Your onboarding confirmation will state which category applies to you.

selfEmployed.title: 2.1 Self-employed status
selfEmployed.content: Where you are a Direct Rider, you are engaged as a self-employed independent contractor. Nothing in these Terms is intended to create an employment relationship, a worker relationship within the meaning of section 230(3)(b) of the Employment Rights Act 1996, a partnership, a joint venture, or an agency relationship between you and Urbandrop.
