independence.title: 2.2 Independence
independence.content: As a Direct Rider you acknowledge that you:
items: operate your own independent delivery business and provide services to multiple clients at your discretion;
decide when, where, and whether to log in to the Platform and accept Deliveries — there is no obligation on you to accept any Delivery, and no obligation on Urbandrop to offer any;

are free to work simultaneously for competing platforms or any other party, including during periods when you are logged in to the Urbandrop rider app, subject only to food-safety and in-progress Delivery obligations;
are responsible for your own tax, National Insurance contributions, and any pension arrangements;
provide your own equipment (including vehicle, mobile device, insulated bag and PPE) save where Urbandrop expressly provides branded items;
bear the economic risk of your delivery activity, including fuel, maintenance, insurance and downtime.

fleetRiders.title: 2.3 Fleet Riders
fleetRiders.content: If you are a Fleet Rider, your primary contractual counterparty is the Fleet Partner. These Terms set out the additional standards and conduct requirements that apply when you handle an Urbandrop Delivery. In the event of conflict between these Terms and your agreement with the Fleet Partner on matters specific to the Platform (e.g., food safety, use of the rider app, Customer data), these Terms take precedence solely in respect of that Delivery.

plainEnglish.title: Status in Plain English
plainEnglish.content: You are your own boss. You decide when to log in, you can send a substitute, and you can work for our competitors at the same time. We don't tell you how to do your job — we tell you what standards we expect for the Customer's order. That's the deal.

minimumEligibility.title: 3.1 Minimum eligibility
minimumEligibility.content: To be approved as a Rider, you must:
items: be aged 18 or over;
have the legal right to work in the United Kingdom (see Clause 4);
hold a valid UK bank account in your own name or in the name of your registered business;
hold a valid National Insurance number (for Direct Riders);
be physically able to carry and handle grocery loads of up to 15 kg safely;
own or have exclusive lawful use of a vehicle compliant with Clause 5;
own a smartphone capable of running the latest two major versions of iOS or Android, with mobile data and location services available.

documents.title: 3.2 Documents required
documents.content: As part of onboarding you must provide clear, unexpired copies of:
items: proof of identity (UK or Irish passport, biometric residence permit, or share code issued by the Home Office);
proof of right to work in the UK;
proof of address dated within the last three (3) months;
for motorised vehicles: full UK driving licence, V5 registration document, valid MOT (where applicable), and fully comprehensive motor insurance with hire & reward ("delivery") cover;
for e-bikes and cycles: confirmation that the vehicle complies with the Electrically Assisted Pedal Cycles Regulations 1983 (max 250W motor, assistance cut-off at 15.5 mph / 25 km/h);
a recent passport-style photograph for account verification;
bank details for payment (account name, sort code, account number);
where applicable, your UTR (Unique Taxpayer Reference) number or VAT registration number.

dbs.title: 3.3 DBS and background checks
dbs.content: Urbandrop reserves the right to require a Basic Disclosure check from the Disclosure and Barring Service (or Disclosure Scotland / AccessNI equivalent), and to refuse or revoke onboarding where a conviction or caution is materially relevant to the safe performance of delivery services — for example unspent convictions for violence, sexual offences, theft, fraud, or serious driving offences. Refusal or revocation decisions may be appealed in writing within 14 days of notification.

identityVerification.title: 3.4 Identity verification
identityVerification.content: We use identity-verification technology (including facial recognition and live-selfie matching) at onboarding and periodically throughout your engagement. You consent to this processing as a condition of accessing the Platform. Details of how this data is handled are set out in our Privacy Policy and in Clause 13 below.

falseInfo.title: 3.5 False or misleading information
falseInfo.content: Providing false, forged or misleading information (including using someone else's identity, document, or likeness) is grounds for immediate and permanent deactivation, forfeiture of outstanding fees accrued fraudulently, and — where appropriate — referral to HMRC, the Home Office and/or the police.

rightToWork.title: 4.1 Right to Work in the UK
rightToWork.content1: Since 1 March 2025, Right to Work checks apply to all workers in the gig economy, regardless of employment status. You warrant that you have, and will maintain throughout your engagement, the lawful right to work in the United Kingdom. You must notify Urbandrop immediately if your immigration status changes, if your visa or BRP expires, or if the Home Office imposes any condition affecting your right to work.
rightToWork.content2: Urbandrop is entitled to repeat Right to Work checks at any reasonable interval and to suspend your account pending verification. Engaging an illegal worker exposes Urbandrop to civil penalties of up to £60,000 per worker and criminal sanctions; we therefore apply these checks strictly and without exception.

substitution.title: 4.2 Right of substitution
substitution.content: As a self-employed Direct Rider, you have the right to appoint another individual (a "Substitute") to perform a Delivery in your place. This right is genuine and unfettered, subject only to the following reasonable conditions which exist solely to protect Customers and meet our legal obligations:
items: the Substitute must be aged 18 or over;
the Substitute must have the lawful right to work in the UK, and you must verify this before allowing them to perform the Delivery;
the Substitute must comply with the food safety and vehicle requirements set out in these Terms;
you remain responsible for the Delivery, for payment to your Substitute, and for the Substitute's conduct;

you are prohibited from renting, selling or commercially transferring your Rider Account, or from receiving payment in exchange for providing Platform access to others. Substitution is not a licence to operate a rider-account rental business.

accountRental.title: Account Rental is Prohibited
accountRental.content: Following reported exploitation in the UK gig economy — including a 2024 case in Bristol involving the renting out of delivery accounts to unchecked riders — Urbandrop operates a zero-tolerance policy on account sharing for profit. Genuine substitution on a one-off or occasional basis is permitted. Systematic rental of your account to others is a material breach of these Terms and will result in permanent deactivation, forfeiture of earnings, and (where appropriate) referral to the Home Office, HMRC and police.

standards.title: 5.1 Vehicle standards
standards.content: Your vehicle must be roadworthy, legally compliant, and suitable for the safe carriage of food. Specifically:
items: Bicycles and e-bikes must comply with the Electrically Assisted Pedal Cycles Regulations 1983. Throttle-only e-bikes, de-restricted e-bikes, and any e-bike capable of self-propulsion above 15.5 mph (25 km/h) are classified as motor vehicles and require insurance, tax, registration, helmet and licence compliance on the same basis as a motorbike.
Motorbikes, scooters and mopeds require a valid CBT or full licence, road tax, MOT (where applicable), and motor insurance including hire & reward cover.
Cars and vans require a valid UK driving licence, road tax, MOT, and motor insurance including hire & reward cover.

insurance.title: 5.2 Insurance
insurance.content1: You are solely responsible for maintaining appropriate insurance for your vehicle and your delivery activity. Standard social, domestic & pleasure motor policies do not cover paid delivery work. You must hold hire & reward cover (sometimes called "courier" or "delivery" cover) for motorised vehicles.
insurance.content2: For cyclists and e-bike riders, we strongly recommend third-party liability insurance (commonly available from British Cycling, Cycling UK, or a dedicated courier policy) to protect you and any Customer against injury or property damage.
insurance.content3: Urbandrop does not provide you with motor, cycle or income-protection insurance. You must provide evidence of valid insurance at onboarding and at any point on reasonable request.

equipment.title: 5.3 Equipment
items: a clean, food-grade insulated delivery bag of sufficient size to keep chilled and ambient items separate where required;
appropriate PPE for your mode of transport — including, for motorised and pedal riders, a correctly fitted helmet meeting British Standard BS EN 1078 (cycle) or ECE 22.06 (motorbike);
high-visibility clothing in hours of darkness or reduced visibility (Road Traffic Act 1988 duty of care);
lights compliant with the Road Vehicles Lighting Regulations 1989 at night.

brandedEquipment.title: 5.4 Urbandrop-branded equipment
brandedEquipment.content: Where Urbandrop provides you with branded equipment (e.g., insulated bags, jackets), it remains our property, is loaned to you for use during your engagement, and must be returned on termination in reasonable condition (fair wear and tear excepted). A reasonable replacement charge may apply where equipment is lost, damaged or not returned, and will be disclosed at the point of issue.

foodSafety.title: 06 Food Safety & Handling
foodSafety.content: Urbandrop delivers food. The Food Safety Act 1990, the Food Hygiene (England) Regulations 2013 (and the equivalent regulations in Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland), and the Food Information Regulations 2014 apply to every Delivery. You must:
items: maintain personal hygiene appropriate to the handling of food — clean hands, clean clothes, no smoking, vaping or eating during collection or transit;
not perform Deliveries while suffering from any condition likely to contaminate food (for example Norovirus, gastroenteritis, open skin lesions on hands);
keep the insulated bag clean and free from contamination, and not use it for anything other than food or grocery Deliveries;
maintain the cold chain — chilled and frozen items must be kept separately insulated and delivered promptly; under Food Standards Agency guidance, chilled items should remain below 8°C in transit;
never open, inspect, rearrange, or remove items from a sealed order; tamper-evident seals must remain intact from Merchant to Customer;
never consume, sample, taste or otherwise interfere with any item in an order, including "missing" or "cancelled" items;
handle allergens with care — do not transport orders alongside any personal items, pets, strong-smelling substances, or anything that may cause cross-contamination;
report immediately any incident that may affect food safety (spillage, collision, bag contamination) via the rider app.

allergenDuty.title: Allergen Duty
allergenDuty.content: The Food Information Regulations 2014 require accurate information on the fourteen major allergens to reach the Customer. Any tampering with packaging, substitution of items, or loss of allergen labelling during transit may cause serious harm and is a strict-liability breach of these Terms.

acceptance.title: 7.1 Acceptance
acceptance.content: When a Delivery is offered through the rider app you may accept or decline it at your discretion. Declining a Delivery does not of itself affect your standing, though repeated unresponsiveness after accepting a Delivery (see 7.4) may.

collection.title: 7.2 Collection
collection.content: On arriving at the Merchant you must identify yourself clearly, confirm the correct order, and check the packaging appears intact and appropriate for the journey. You must report any obvious issue (missing items the Merchant has flagged, damaged packaging, spill, open seal) through the rider app before departure.

transit.title: 7.3 Transit
transit.content: You must take the safest reasonable route to the Customer and drive, ride or cycle in accordance with the Highway Code and all applicable laws. You must not use the rider app in a way that breaches Regulation 110 of the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986 (mobile-phone use while driving).

handover.title: 7.4 Handover
handover.content: Unless the Customer has given pre-arranged "leave-at-door" instructions:
items: hand the Delivery directly to the Customer or an adult at the delivery address;

verify age for any age-restricted item via the rider app's ID-check flow — you must not release alcohol, tobacco, vapes or knives to anyone appearing under 25 without valid photo ID (Challenge 25 policy);
obtain photo confirmation where instructed;

if no one is available and no pre-authorised leave-at-door instruction exists, contact the Customer through the in-app function and follow the escalation prompts; do not leave perishable items in unsafe locations.

prohibited.title: 7.5 Prohibited conduct
items: contacting the Customer outside the Platform's in-app messaging except in a genuine delivery emergency;
making unsolicited advances, comments on appearance, or any form of harassment;
attending the delivery address at a later time for non-delivery purposes;
photographing or recording the Customer, their home or belongings beyond the confirmation photo required by the app;
soliciting, accepting or offering bribes, kickbacks or off-platform payments from Customers or Merchants;
marking a Delivery as complete when it has not been delivered.

deliveryFees.title: 8.1 Delivery fees (Direct Riders)
deliveryFees.content: Each Delivery offered to you through the rider app will display a headline fee calculated on a transparent basis set out in the Rider Fee Schedule. Components may include a base pickup fee, a distance component, a time-in-transit component, and dynamic adjustments during peak demand or adverse weather. By accepting a Delivery you agree to the displayed fee for that Delivery.

tips.title: 8.2 Tips
tips.content: Customer tips paid through the Platform are passed to you in full. Urbandrop does not retain any portion of a Customer tip and, in line with the Employment (Allocation of Tips) Act 2023 where applicable, tips are not used to offset the headline fee. Cash tips offered directly by a Customer belong to you.

settlement.title: 8.3 Settlement and payment
items: Fees are settled weekly, paid by BACS transfer to your nominated UK bank account, typically on the Tuesday following the end of the week (Monday 00:01 – Sunday 23:59 UTC) in which the Deliveries were completed.

You will receive a self-billed invoice through the rider app itemising each Delivery, tips, adjustments and any deductions. This facility is provided for your administrative convenience; it does not affect your self-employed status.
If you dispute any item on a self-billed invoice, raise it via the in-app support channel within 30 days. After 30 days the invoice is deemed accepted.

deductions.title: 8.4 Deductions
deductions.content: Urbandrop may deduct from amounts owed to you:
items: sums paid in error or as a result of fraud or manipulation;
charges for lost, damaged or unreturned Urbandrop-branded equipment, subject to prior disclosure;
legitimate refunds to Customers or Merchants where a failure or error is directly attributable to your conduct (e.g., missing items due to tampering, undelivered orders marked as delivered).
deductions.note: Deductions will be itemised and notified. You may challenge any deduction under Clause 17.

vat.title: 8.5 VAT
vat.content: If you are VAT-registered, you must provide your VAT registration number so that self-billed invoices are compliant with the VAT Regulations 1995. You are responsible for your own VAT returns.

tax.title: 09 Tax & National Insurance
tax.content: As a self-employed Direct Rider, you are responsible for registering for Self Assessment with HMRC and for paying:
items: Income Tax on your delivery profits;
Class 2 National Insurance Contributions where your profits exceed the small-profits threshold;
Class 4 NICs where your profits exceed the lower profits limit;
VAT, if your taxable turnover exceeds the VAT registration threshold in a rolling 12-month period.
tax.content2: Urbandrop does not operate PAYE for Direct Riders and does not deduct income tax or National Insurance at source. Under the Online Platforms Reporting Rules (OECD Model, implemented in the UK from 1 January 2024), Urbandrop is required to report rider income and identifying details to HMRC on an annual basis; you are deemed to consent to this reporting as a condition of using the Platform.
tax.content3: You are responsible for keeping appropriate records of your business income and allowable expenses and for filing a Self Assessment tax return by 31 January each year.

notTaxAdvice.title: Not Tax Advice
notTaxAdvice.content: This Clause is a summary, not tax advice. If you are unsure about your obligations, consult HMRC directly at gov.uk/self-assessment-tax-returns or an independent accountant.

ratings.title: 10.1 Customer ratings
ratings.content: After each Delivery, Customers may rate their experience on a five-star scale. Ratings help us, Merchants and you maintain service quality, but a single poor rating does not, on its own, affect your account standing.

metrics.title: 10.2 Operational metrics
metrics.content: Urbandrop monitors a small number of objective metrics:

table.onTimeDesc: % of Deliveries arriving within the app's estimated window

howWeUse.title: 10.3 How we use these metrics
howWeUse.content: Metrics are used to identify Riders who may benefit from support (additional guidance materials, equipment refresh, route-tooling tips) and, in cases of persistent poor performance after support, to consider suspension or deactivation under Clause 11. You can see your own metrics in the rider app.

whatWeDontDo.title: 10.4 What we don't do
whatWeDontDo.content: Ratings and metrics do not dictate the Deliveries you are offered, your pay per Delivery, or your order of priority in the matching system. Urbandrop does not use psychological nudges, forfeiture mechanics, or ratings-based "gamification" to pressure Riders into working longer hours.

temporary.title: 11.1 Temporary suspension
temporary.content: Urbandrop may temporarily suspend your access to the Platform where we have a reasonable, good-faith basis to believe that:
items: there is a material risk to Customer safety, Merchant safety, or another Rider's safety;
your Right to Work status, identity documentation, vehicle documentation or insurance requires re-verification;
a serious complaint has been received that warrants investigation;
there is apparent misuse of the Platform (account rental, fraudulent Delivery completion, collusion).
temporary.note: We will give you the reason for suspension in writing (via the rider app or email) as soon as reasonably practicable and, save where law enforcement requires otherwise, an opportunity to respond.

permanent.title: 11.2 Permanent deactivation
permanent.content: We may permanently deactivate your Rider Account for, among other reasons:
items: fraud, theft, or misappropriation of a Customer's order;
violence, threatening behaviour, harassment or discrimination;
serious breach of food safety obligations;
loss of the legal right to work in the UK;
driving or cycling offences of a nature materially relevant to delivery work;
account-rental or sharing in breach of Clause 4.2;
persistent material breach of these Terms despite prior warning.

fairProcess.title: 11.3 Fair process
fairProcess.content: Except where immediate deactivation is necessary (e.g., to protect safety or comply with law), you will be told the reason, shown the evidence where lawfully possible, and given the opportunity to respond within 14 days. Decisions to deactivate may be escalated to a manager who was not involved in the original decision under Clause 17.

reporting.title: 12.1 Reporting
reporting.content: You must report as soon as reasonably possible, and in any event within 24 hours, any:
items: road traffic collision, regardless of fault or injury;
injury to yourself, a Customer, a Merchant employee, or any third party during a Delivery;
damage to property;
theft or attempted theft of goods or your vehicle during a Delivery;
hate crime, harassment or abusive conduct by a Customer or Merchant.

liability.title: 12.2 Your liability
liability.content: You are responsible for your own acts and omissions. Urbandrop is not liable for damage you cause to third parties, to Customer property, or to your own vehicle or equipment. You must keep yourself insured appropriately (Clause 5.2).

ourLiability.title: 12.3 Our liability to you
ourLiability.content: Subject always to Clause 12.4, Urbandrop's total aggregate liability to you under or in connection with these Terms in any 12-month period is limited to the greater of (a) the total fees paid to you in that period or (b) £1,000. We are not liable for loss of profit, loss of opportunity, loss of goodwill, or any indirect or consequential loss.

liabilityExclusions.title: 12.4 Liability we do not exclude
liabilityExclusions.content: Nothing in these Terms limits or excludes liability for:
items: death or personal injury caused by negligence;
fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation;
any liability which cannot be excluded or limited under applicable law.

emergency.title: 12.5 Emergency contact
emergency.content: If you are involved in a serious incident while working, call 999 first and then notify Urbandrop via the in-app emergency button or the 24-hour rider support line set out in Clause 21.

roles.title: 13.1 Roles
roles.content: For Personal Data you provide to Urbandrop about yourself, Urbandrop is the data controller. For Personal Data about Customers (names, delivery addresses, contact numbers, delivery notes) accessed by you through the rider app, Urbandrop is the controller and you are a data processor acting strictly on Urbandrop's documented instructions for the limited purpose of completing the Delivery.

obligations.title: 13.2 Your obligations as processor
obligations.content: When handling Customer Personal Data you must:
items: use it only to complete the specific Delivery assigned to you;
not store, copy, share, post online, or otherwise retain Customer details after the Delivery is completed;
not contact the Customer outside the Platform's in-app channel;
keep your device and rider app secured with a PIN, passcode or biometric lock;
report a suspected personal data breach to dpo@urbandrop.io within 24 hours.

howWeUse.title: 13.3 How we use your Personal Data
howWeUse.content: We process your Personal Data in accordance with the UK GDPR and our Privacy Policy, which forms part of these Terms. Our lawful bases include performance of this contract, compliance with legal obligations (e.g., Right to Work, HMRC reporting), and legitimate interests in operating the Platform safely.

rights.title: 13.4 Your rights
rights.content: You have the rights of a data subject under UK GDPR — access, rectification, erasure (subject to legal retention), restriction, portability, and objection. To exercise any of these, contact dpo@urbandrop.io. You may also complain to the Information Commissioner's Office (ico.org.uk).

ip.title: 14 Intellectual Property
ip.content: All intellectual property rights in the Platform, the rider app, Urbandrop trademarks, logos, branding, training materials and documentation remain the property of Urbandrop or our licensors. You are granted a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable licence to use the rider app solely for the purpose of performing Deliveries.
items: reverse-engineer, decompile or attempt to derive source code from the rider app;
scrape, copy or otherwise misappropriate data, order flows, pricing or routing information;
use Urbandrop trademarks without our prior written consent;
represent yourself as an employee, agent or partner of Urbandrop beyond what these Terms provide.

confidentiality.title: 15 Confidentiality
confidentiality.content1: You may come across confidential information about Urbandrop, Customers, Merchants or other Riders in the course of your engagement — including pricing structures, order patterns, delivery routes, rider-app features not yet public, and Customer details. You must keep this information confidential during and after your engagement and must not disclose it to any third party except as required by law.
confidentiality.content2: Confidentiality does not apply to information that is, or lawfully becomes, public through no breach by you, or that you lawfully possessed before your engagement.

health.title: 16 Health, Safety & Wellbeing
health.content: Although you are not an employee, we take Rider safety seriously and, to the extent the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974 applies to our operations, we will conduct our business in a way that does not expose you to risks so far as is reasonably practicable.

responsibilities.title: 16.1 Your responsibilities
items: never deliver while under the influence of alcohol or drugs (legal or illegal) that impair your ability to ride or drive safely;

take adequate rest — do not exceed a reasonable working pattern that compromises safe delivery (we recommend breaks of at least 15 minutes every 2 hours, and we will not penalise you for taking them);
decline or end a Delivery that becomes unsafe due to weather, vehicle fault, or personal condition;
wear appropriate weather, reflective and protective clothing.

wellbeing.title: 16.2 Wellbeing support
items: access to a confidential 24/7 mental health helpline (details in the rider app);
signposting to national resources including Samaritans (116 123) and Mind (0300 123 3393);
a clear no-retaliation policy — raising a safety concern, reporting a Customer's abusive behaviour, or declining an unsafe Delivery will not adversely affect your standing on the Platform.

equality.title: 16.3 Equality, respect and anti-harassment
equality.content: Urbandrop has zero tolerance for discrimination, harassment or victimisation on grounds of a protected characteristic under the Equality Act 2010 — whether by Riders, Customers, Merchants or Urbandrop staff. Reports are taken seriously, investigated confidentially, and acted on.

raise.title: 17.1 Raise it with us first
raise.content: If you have a complaint — about a fee, a deduction, a suspension, another user's conduct, or anything else — please raise it through the rider app's support channel or email riders@urbandrop.io. We aim to acknowledge within two (2) working days and substantively respond within ten (10) working days.

escalation.title: 17.2 Escalation
escalation.content: If you are not satisfied with our response you may escalate to an independent reviewer within Urbandrop (not involved in the original decision) by emailing appeals@urbandrop.io. Escalation decisions are final as to Urbandrop's internal process.

independentRedress.title: 17.3 Independent redress
independentRedress.content: Nothing in these Terms prevents you from bringing a claim in the courts of England and Wales or from contacting a regulator (including HMRC, the Information Commissioner's Office, or the Health & Safety Executive) where appropriate.

changes.title: 18 Changes to these Terms
changes.content: UrbanDrop may update these Terms from time to time. We will notify you of material changes via the rider app, email, or SMS at least 14 days before they take effect. Your continued use of the Platform after the update date constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms. If you do not agree to the changes, you may terminate this agreement by giving 7 days' written notice.

contact.title: 21 Contact
contact.content: For questions, support, or to serve notices under these Terms:

governingLaw.title: 20 Governing Law & Jurisdiction
governingLaw.content: These Terms are governed by the laws of England and Wales. The courts of England and Wales have exclusive jurisdiction. If any provision of these Terms is held to be invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions continue in full force and effect.

entireAgreement.title: 21 Entire Agreement
entireAgreement.content: These Terms, together with the Privacy Policy and any additional agreements incorporated by reference, constitute the entire agreement between you and UrbanDrop relating to your use of the Platform and supersede all prior agreements, representations, or understandings, whether written or oral.
